sourceMovie.title: Fright Night
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Fright Night works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: What We Do in the Shadows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fright Night clicked for you, What We Do in the Shadows (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Day Shift
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fright Night clicked for you, Day Shift (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fright Night?
faq.answer: Try What We Do in the Shadows, Day Shift, The Fearless Vampire Killers—each shares DNA with Fright Night while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fright Night?
faq.answer: Fright Night (2011) is best described as Horror, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fright Night part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Fright Night (Reboot) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
